questions.question: Why does Euclid’s Division Algorithm always terminate?
questions.answer: Because the remainders keep decreasing and cannot be negative.

questions.question: Why is √p irrational for any prime p?
questions.answer: Because assuming it rational leads to a contradiction.

questions.question: Why is uniqueness of prime factorisation important?
questions.answer: It ensures that no number has more than one prime factorisation.

questions.question: Why does contradiction prove a statement true?
questions.answer: Because the opposite assumption leads to an impossible situation.

questions.question: Why can √2 not be written in lowest fractional form?
questions.answer: Because both numerator and denominator become divisible by 2.

questions.question: Why does HCF × LCM formula fail for three numbers?
questions.answer: Because the relation holds only for two numbers.

questions.question: Why must remainder be less than the divisor?
questions.answer: To ensure meaningful division.

questions.question: What contradiction arises in the proof of √3 irrationality?
questions.answer: Both numerator and denominator become divisible by 3.

questions.question: Why does p | a² imply p | a?
questions.answer: Because of the Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic.

questions.question: Why is √9 rational but √8 irrational?
questions.answer: Because 9 is a perfect square while 8 is not.

questions.question: Why is 1 neither prime nor composite?
questions.answer: Because it has only one factor.

questions.question: Why are coprime numbers important in proofs?
questions.answer: They help establish contradictions in irrationality proofs.

questions.question: Why do some rational numbers have repeating decimals?
questions.answer: Because their denominators have primes other than 2 and 5.

questions.question: Why is √5 irrational?
questions.answer: Because assuming it rational leads to a contradiction.

questions.question: Why is 2 the only even prime number?
questions.answer: Because all other even numbers are divisible by 2.

questions.question: Why must prime factorisation use only primes?
questions.answer: Because primes are the basic building blocks of numbers.

questions.question: Why is √8 irrational?
questions.answer: Because it simplifies to 2√2, which is irrational.

questions.question: Why does terminating decimal imply factors 2 and 5?
questions.answer: Because only powers of 10 produce terminating decimals.

questions.question: Why is 0 a rational number?
questions.answer: Because it can be written as 0/1.

questions.question: Why is π irrational?
questions.answer: Because its decimal expansion is non-terminating and non-repeating.

questions.question: Why does irrational × rational remain irrational?
questions.answer: Because the irrationality cannot be eliminated.

questions.question: Why is √49 rational?
questions.answer: Because √49 = 7, which is an integer.

questions.question: Why does contradiction invalidate assumptions?
questions.answer: Because a true assumption cannot lead to false results.

questions.question: Why are real numbers important?
questions.answer: They represent all points on the number line.

questions.question: Why does Euclid’s algorithm depend on decreasing remainders?
questions.answer: Because it ensures the process ends at zero.
